course.title: Build a Production-Grade Pokedex in Go
course.description: Go learners ready to integrate commands, HTTP, typed JSON, state, caching, concurrency, cancellation, persistence, security, testing, packaging, and operations into one cumulative user-facing product. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative accessible repl pokedex using pokéapi resource, pagination, pokemon, and location-area contracts through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate go ownership, typed json, reusable http clients, fair-use caching, duplicate suppression, goroutines, cancellation, persistence, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled go compiler, httptest, race, fuzz, filesystem, signal, package, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative performance defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

credential.title: Build a Production-Grade Pokedex in Go Certificate

modules.title: Field-Guide Outcomes, Command Language, and REPL Contract
modules.description: A ranger team asks for a Pokedex but has not defined who uses it, which decisions it supports, what commands exist, how sessions end, or what evidence makes the command useful. Learners cumulatively produce bounded field-guide product and REPL charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define learner, field user, decision, command sequence, accepted latency, failure consequence, and observable value before coding.
Specify command names, argument grammar, whitespace policy, help text, aliases, unknown-command behavior, and stable output contracts.
Model start, prompt, read, parse, dispatch, result, continue, EOF, quit, cancellation, and cleanup as explicit session states.

modules.title: Repository Baseline, Go 1.26 Toolchain, Modules, and Reproduction
modules.description: The prototype runs from an unknown revision, uses an unrecorded Go toolchain, has no module identity, relies on warm caches, and cannot be rebuilt from a clean checkout. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
Record Go 1.26.5, GOOS, GOARCH, GOTOOLCHAIN, module mode, build tags, and supported platform matrix.
Choose module path, command and internal package boundaries, dependency direction, and import visibility before growth.

modules.title: Input Reading, Tokenization, Command Registry, and Dispatch
modules.description: The loop assumes every read succeeds, splits arguments inconsistently, indexes missing tokens, hard-codes a growing switch, and cannot test dispatch without real stdin and stdout. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic command parser and injected dispatcher through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose bufio.Reader or Scanner from line-size, token, error, cancellation, and testability requirements and configure any explicit limit.
Normalize line endings and whitespace under a documented grammar without corrupting names or silently accepting extra arguments.
Validate required, optional, repeated, missing, and extra arguments before handler access and return actionable usage.

modules.title: Help, Result Formatting, Streams, and Terminal Accessibility
modules.description: Commands mix data and prose, errors appear on stdout, help order changes across runs, long records become unreadable, and progress depends on color and cursor motion. Learners cumulatively produce screen-reader-friendly deterministic terminal presenter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Generate concise overview and per-command help from one registry while showing syntax, examples, defaults, and exit paths.
Assign data, diagnostics, progress, and usage to deliberate streams and return stable command outcomes or exit statuses.
Sort maps and sets, label units, preserve stable field order, and distinguish absent, zero, empty, and unknown values.

modules.title: PokéAPI Resource Identity, URL Construction, Pagination, and Fair Use
modules.description: The client concatenates user input into arbitrary URLs, treats list and detail resources alike, ignores pagination links, and sends repeated requests despite the API fair-use cache request. Learners cumulatively produce fixed-origin PokéAPI resource and fair-use policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.
Distinguish resource type, numeric ID, canonical lowercase name, list query, and response self URL without unsafe equivalence.
Construct path segments and limit or offset queries with net/url while rejecting slash, control, and oversized input.

modules.title: JSON Decoding, DTOs, Domain Types, Unknown Fields, and Schema Drift
modules.description: The program decodes into map[string]any, asserts nested types, confuses absent with zero, trusts response identity, and breaks when harmless unknown fields appear. Learners cumulatively produce typed PokéAPI DTO-to-domain admission boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define narrow transport structs with exact JSON tags, nested shapes, optionality, and only fields needed by the command.
Choose unknown-field tolerance deliberately, bound the body before decoding, reject trailing JSON, and preserve decode causes.
Validate ID, name, dimensions, stats, types, abilities, URLs, and collection bounds before constructing domain values.

modules.title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup
modules.description: Each command creates a new client, uses context.Background inside dependencies, ignores non-2xx status, decodes unbounded bodies, and forgets to close responses. Learners cumulatively produce reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Classify success, not found, client, server, redirect, and unexpected status before domain decoding and retain bounded error evidence.

modules.title: Map Command, Page State, Navigation Invariants, and Transactional Updates
modules.description: The map command prints names but loses the page identity, advances before a request succeeds, permits negative offsets, and cannot explain what back should restore. Learners cumulatively produce transactional Pokemon page navigator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state.
Compute candidate navigation, fetch and validate it, then commit state only after success.
Disable or explain next and back at absent-link boundaries and reject negative, excessive, or mismatched pagination state.

modules.title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery
modules.description: Back recomputes offsets instead of restoring observed state, failed requests corrupt history, repeated pages grow memory forever, and concurrent prefetch mutates the visible cursor. Learners cumulatively produce bounded snapshot navigation history through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.

modules.title: Fair-Use Cache Keys, TTL, Clock Injection, Copies, and Eviction
modules.description: The cache keys raw commands, uses time.Now deep inside methods, returns mutable shared slices, never expires failures, and grows without a capacity policy. Learners cumulatively produce bounded TTL resource cache with observable decisions through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Canonicalize endpoint, resource identity, and representation into a typed cache key without mixing user spelling or presentation.
Inject a clock, define freshness at exact boundaries, and distinguish fetched, stored, observed, and expires times.
Choose immutable values, deep copies, or documented ownership so callers cannot mutate cached history or other results.

modules.title: Concurrent Cache Access, Duplicate Suppression, Ownership, and Race Evidence
modules.description: Prefetch and commands access a map without synchronization, duplicate misses launch identical requests, a canceled waiter kills shared work, and tests pass without the race detector. Learners cumulatively produce race-free duplicate-suppressed resource loader through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Protect shared cache invariants with a mutex or owned goroutine and keep lock scope away from slow network work.
Represent one canonical in-flight fetch, waiters, completion, result, and cleanup without leaking entries.
Let each waiter cancel independently while defining when shared underlying work should continue or stop.

modules.title: Pokemon Detail Lookup, Derived Views, Units, and Provenance
modules.description: The inspect command dumps transport JSON, displays raw decimeter and hectogram values without labels, loses API provenance, and panics when optional nested entries are empty. Learners cumulatively produce human-readable validated Pokemon detail view through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Accept numeric ID or constrained canonical name, retain requested and resolved identity, and reject ambiguous or hostile input.
Model stable identity, species, types, abilities, stats, dimensions, and optional fields separately from the transport DTO.
Convert height and weight only under documented API units, label original and displayed units, and test rounding boundaries.

modules.title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence
modules.description: Catch uses a hidden global random source, stores partial response pointers, duplicates the same Pokemon unpredictably, and makes tests depend on luck and request timing. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define whether catch is guaranteed, probabilistic, skill-based, or a collection action and state duplicate and failure semantics.
If probability is used, inject a narrow source, record seed or draw evidence, bound values, and keep policy separate from randomness.
Key owned entries by resolved stable ID while retaining display name and capture evidence without raw-input duplicates.

modules.title: Owned Inspection, Ordering, Release, and Collection Integrity
modules.description: Inspect silently falls back to the network, list order changes with map iteration, release deletes before confirmation, and aliases let cached updates rewrite owned records. Learners cumulatively produce offline inspectable and reversible collection interface through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish owned inspection from remote lookup and return a clear not-owned result without hidden traffic.
Present collection records in a documented stable order with identifiers and totals independent of map iteration.
Validate target, preview the exact owned record, require deliberate confirmation policy, then commit deletion atomically.

modules.title: Location Areas, Encounter Relations, Filtering, and Exploration
modules.description: Explore confuses locations with location areas, requests unbounded encounter data, prints duplicates, ignores empty relations, and loses which area produced each Pokemon. Learners cumulatively produce provenance-preserving location encounter explorer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish region, location, and location-area resources and map the command to the endpoint whose relation answers the user need.
Validate numeric or canonical area identity, retain requested and resolved values, and construct only fixed-origin resource paths.
Deduplicate Pokemon by stable identity while retaining area, version, method, chance, or condition provenance needed by the view.

modules.title: Error Taxonomy, Wrapping, Retry Decisions, User Recovery, and Exit
modules.description: Every failure becomes unable to fetch, handlers log and return nil, retries include invalid input, wrapped causes are discarded, and one command failure terminates the session. Learners cumulatively produce causal recoverable Pokedex error contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify input, not found, protocol, timeout, cancellation, transport, decode, invariant, persistence, and internal errors by caller decision.
Wrap context with percent-w while preserving sentinel or typed matching and avoid exposing unstable internal prose as API.
Retry only explicitly transient idempotent work under attempt, time, spacing, cancellation, and fair-use budgets.

modules.title: Context Propagation, Deadlines, OS Signals, and Graceful Shutdown
modules.description: Handlers create background contexts, Ctrl-C exits before cleanup, signal notifications are never stopped, blocked input and fetch work outlive the session, and cancellation is reported as failure. Learners cumulatively produce signal-aware graceful command lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create one root context with signal.NotifyContext, call its stop function, and define portable interrupt behavior.
Propagate caller context through session, handler, loader, transport, cache wait, and persistence without storing it in long-lived structs.
Reserve cleanup time, derive bounded operation deadlines, and distinguish parent cancellation from local timeout.

modules.title: Bounded Prefetch, Worker Ownership, Ordering, and Backpressure
modules.description: Every map result launches a goroutine, concurrency is unbounded, jobs block after cancellation, output follows completion order, and prefetched failures poison visible state. Learners cumulatively produce bounded cancellable Pokemon prefetch pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define which next-view latency benefit justifies prefetch traffic and when no prefetch should occur.
Set worker, queue, request, byte, and time ceilings from fair-use and resource evidence rather than input size.
Assign which goroutine creates, sends, closes, drains, cancels, and waits for each channel and result.

modules.title: Collection Persistence, Versioned Schemas, Atomic Replacement, and Restore
modules.description: The program writes live maps directly to the final file, trusts arbitrary JSON on load, has no schema version, loses permissions on replacement, and cannot recover from interruption. Learners cumulatively produce versioned atomic offline collection store through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep browser lessons on pure byte models and place native filesystem effects behind an injected save and load port.
Define version, application identity, timestamp policy, records, provenance, and checksum or integrity scope with deterministic encoding.
Bound bytes, decode exactly one document, validate schema and every record, reject duplicates, and preserve the last valid in-memory state on error.

modules.title: URL Allowlisting, Untrusted API Data, Terminal Safety, Files, and Resource Abuse
modules.description: The client follows redirects anywhere, prints control characters from remote data, accepts arbitrary save paths, logs full responses, and lets crafted records exhaust memory or terminal output. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Revalidate scheme, host, port, redirect count, and destination policy on every hop and reject user-controlled egress.
Normalize or visibly escape control, bidi, and ANSI sequences from API, file, and user data before terminal presentation.
Choose application-owned storage roots, reject traversal or unexpected file types, and minimize permission and symlink assumptions.

modules.title: Unit, Integration, Fuzz, Race, Leak, and CLI Behavior Evidence
modules.description: Tests call the live API, share cache state, assert implementation details, never run the binary, ignore fuzz seeds and race findings, and pass despite goroutine leaks. Learners cumulatively produce layered deterministic Go verification harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Assign pure domain, handler, cache, transport, persistence, subprocess, race, fuzz, and controlled live-smoke evidence to separate risks.
Use named table cases with minimal original fixtures, changed inputs, explicit wants, and isolated setup without loop-variable mistakes.
Prefer narrow fakes for decisions and httptest for real HTTP boundaries while asserting requests, responses, lifecycle, and faults.

modules.title: Telemetry, Latency, Capacity, Cache Effectiveness, and Fair-Use Operations
modules.description: Logs contain full URLs and bodies, metrics use Pokemon names as labels, average latency hides stalls, cache hit rate lacks workload context, and optimization increases API traffic. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-minimized capacity and fair-use evidence report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Correlate command, operation, cache decision, request class, attempt, status class, duration, outcome, and cleanup without sensitive or unbounded fields.
Use low-cardinality counters and distributions for commands, outcomes, cache, requests, bytes, latency, cancellation, and queue depth.
Measure representative cold and warm command latency with percentile, workload, environment, sample, and correctness context.

modules.title: Command Packaging, Build Identity, Cross-Platform Behavior, and Distribution
modules.description: The binary works only from the repository root, embeds no version identity, writes beside itself, assumes Unix signals and paths, and ships an untested archive from a dirty tree. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible cross-platform Go Pokedex artifact through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep cmd entrypoint thin, internal packages directional, fixtures separate, and runtime paths independent of source checkout.
Expose semantic version, revision, build time policy, Go version, platform, and dirty-state evidence without making builds unnecessarily nondeterministic.
Test path, newline, signal, terminal, rename, permission, and architecture assumptions on supported platforms and document differences.

modules.title: End-to-End Release, Incident Recovery, and Engineering Defense
modules.description: A candidate passes unit tests but lacks clean-build, race, accessibility, fair-use, failure, migration, rollback, restore, and stakeholder evidence, while residual risks have no owners. Learners cumulatively produce defensible recoverable Go Pokedex release dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Reconcile source, module graph, fixtures, tests, binary, command behavior, saved data, checksum, documentation, and stakeholder acceptance to one revision.
Rehearse invalid input, API not found, timeout, malformed response, cancellation, cache corruption, write interruption, disk failure, and signal shutdown.
Prove forward migration, old-binary compatibility limits, backup, rollback, restored collection integrity, and support decision points.
